Chhetri and his knights make it a momentous night for India |url=https://www.the-aiff.com/article/champions-chhetri-and-his-knights-make-it-a-momentous-night-for-india |access-date=18 June 2023 |publisher=[[All India Football Federation]]}}</ref> reached the semi-finals of the [[2023 SAFF Championship]],<ref name="SAFF23">{{Cite web |date=1 July 2023 |title=India shoot their way into final as Lebanon pay the penalty |url=https://www.the-aiff.com/article/india-shoot-their-way-into-final-as-lebanon-pay-the-penalty |access-date=2 July 2023 |publisher=[[All India Football Federation]]}}</ref> and placed third at the [[2023 King's Cup]].<ref name="kings23">{{Cite web |last=Palshikar |first=Prathamesh |date=10 September 2023 |title=King's Cup 2023 football: India lose 1–0 to Lebanon in third-place playoff |url=https://olympics.com/en/news/kings-cup-2023-football-third-place-playoff-india-vs-lebanon-result-scores |access-date=23 October 2023 |publisher=[[Olympic Games]]}}</ref> Radulović returned as head coach ahead of the tournament, held in January 2024.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Atta |first=Ashraf Hamed |date=11 December 2023 |title=Coach Radulovic returns to Lebanon job a month before Asian Cup |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/coach-radulovic-returns-lebanon-job-month-before-asian-cup-2023-12-11/ |access-date=19 February 2024 |publisher=[[Reuters]]}}</ref> Lebanon were drawn in [[2023 AFC Asian Cup Group A|Group A]] with hosts and defending champions Qatar, China, and debutants [[Tajikistan national football team|Tajikistan]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Church |first=Michael |date=11 May 2023 |title=Holders Qatar to face China, Tajikistan and Lebanon at Asian Cup |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/holders-qatar-face-china-tajikistan-lebanon-asian-cup-2023-05-11/ |access-date=30 January 2024 |publisher=[[Reuters]]}}</ref> They played the tournament's opening match at the [[Lusail Stadium]] in front of 82,490 spectators, losing 3–0 to Qatar.<ref name="QAT24">{{Cite web |last=McKirdy |first=Andrew |date=13 January 2024 |title=Qatar puts World Cup heartache behind it to win Asian Cup opener |url=https://www.japantimes.co.jp/sports/2024/01/13/soccer/qatar-wins-lebanon-asian-cup/ |access-date=30 January 2024 |publisher=[[The Japan Times]]}}</ref> Lebanon then held China to a goalless draw, leaving qualification to the knockout stage dependent on the final match against Tajikistan.<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 January 2024 |title=China and Lebanon settle for 0–0 draw to leave Asian Cup progress in the balance |url=https://www.newarab.com/news/china-and-lebanon-settle-0-0-draw-asian-cup |access-date=30 January 2024 |publisher=[[The New Arab]]}}</ref> [[Bassel Jradi]] gave Lebanon the lead early in the second half, but [[Kassem El Zein]] was sent off in the 52nd minute.<ref name="TJK24">{{Cite web |date=22 January 2024 |title=Tajikistan 2–1 Lebanon – Highlights |url=https://www.beinsports.com/en-mena/football/afc-asian-cup-qatar-2023-/articles-video/tajikistan-2-1-lebanon-highlights-2024-01-22 |access-date=30 January 2024 |publisher=[[beIN Sports]]}}</ref> Tajikistan scored twice late on to win 2–1, eliminating Lebanon from the competition as they finished bottom of the group with one point.<ref name="TJK24" />

===2024–present: Failed Asian Cup qualification===
In the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|second round of qualification]] for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026 World Cup]], Lebanon were drawn with Australia, Palestine and Bangladesh.<ref>{{Cite web |date=27 July 2023 |title=AFC qualifying draw made for FIFA World Cup 26 |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/articles/afc-qualifying-draw-made-for-fifa-world-cup-26 |access-date=13 June 2024 |publisher=[[FIFA]]}}</ref> Following one win, three draws, and one defeat,<ref>{{Cite web |title=FIFA World Cup 26 – AFC Qualifiers – Standings |url=https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/qualifiers/afc/standings |access-date=13 June 2024 |publisher=[[FIFA]]}}</ref> Lebanon's third-place finish advanced them to the [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|final round of qualification]] for the [[2027 AFC Asian Cup|2027 Asian Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=11 June 2024 |script-title=ar:المنتخب اللبناني يتغلب على بنغلاديش ويبلغ ملحق تصفيات كأس آسيا |trans-title=The Lebanon national team defeats Bangladesh and reaches the Asian Cup qualifiers |url=https://staddoha.com/latest-news/%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%85%D9%86%D8%AA%D8%AE%D8%A8-%D8%A7%D9%84%D9%84%D8%A8%D9%86%D8%A7%D9%86%D9%8A-%D9%8A%D8%AA%D8%BA%D9%84%D8%A8-%D8%B9%D9%84%D9%89-%D8%A8%D9%86%D8%BA%D9%84%D8%A7%D8%AF%D9%8A%D8%B4-%D9%88/ |website=Stad Al Doha |language=ar}}</ref> The World Cup qualifiers were also the last games of Lebanon's captain Hassan Maatouk, who retired as the team's all-time top goalscorer and most-capped player with 26 goals in 123 games.<ref>{{Cite web |date=11 June 2024 |script-title=ar:معتوق يقود لبنان الى فوز كبير بـ "هاتريك" في مباراته الوداعية |trans-title=Maatouk leads Lebanon to a big victory with a hat-trick in his farewell match |url=https://the-lfa.com.lb/news/Asian_qual_m6.php |access-date=11 June 2024 |publisher=[[Lebanese Football Association]] |language=ar}}</ref>

In the final round of Asian Cup qualification, Lebanon were drawn alongside [[Yemen national football team|Yemen]], [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]], and [[Brunei national football team|Brunei]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 December 2024 |title=Saudi Arabia 2027 Qualifiers – Know Your Teams: Groups A–C |url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/afc_asian_cup.html/news/saudi-arabia-2027-qualifiers-know-your-teams-groups-a-c |access-date=5 June 2026 |publisher=[[Asian Football Confederation]]}}</ref> After five matches, the team led their group with 13 points, recording four wins and one draw,<ref>{{Cite web |date=18 November 2025 |title=AFC Asian Cup Qualifiers – Group B: Brunei Darussalam 0–3 Lebanon |url=https://www.the-afc.com/en/national/afc_asian_cup.html/news/qualifiers-group-b-brunei-darussalam-0-3-lebanon |access-date=5 June 2026 |publisher=[[Asian Football Confederation]]}}</ref> and headed into the final match against Yemen requiring only a draw to secure qualification for the tournament.<ref name="gahwashi">{{Cite web |last=Hamid |first=Ashraf |date=5 June 2026 |title=Al Gahwashi double seals Yemen's Asian Cup berth at Lebanon’s expense |url=https://www.reuters.com/sports/soccer/al-gahwashi-double-seals-yemens-asian-cup-berth-lebanons-expense-2026-06-04/ |access-date=5 June 2026 |publisher=[[Reuters]]}}</ref> In November 2025, Lebanon failed [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup qualification|qualification]] for the [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup|2025 Arab Cup]] after a defeat to [[Sudan national football team|Sudan]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=26 November 2025 |title=10-man Sudan ejects Lebanon in FIFA Arab Cup |url=https://cecafaonline.com/10-man-sudan-ejects-lebanon-in-fifa-arab-cup-2025/ |access-date=5 June 2026 |publisher=[[CECAFA]]}}</ref> Following this result, head coach Radulović was dismissed and replaced in 2026 by Algerian manager [[Madjid Bougherra]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=28 January 2026 |title=Football: Majid Bougherra appointed new head coach of Lebanon |url=https://today.lorientlejour.com/article/1493113/football-majid-bougherra-appointed-new-head-coach-of-lebanon.html |access-date=5 June 2026 |publisher=[[L'Orient–Le Jour]]}}</ref> The final Asian Cup qualifying match, originally scheduled for March 2026, was postponed to June 2026 due to security concerns arising from the [[2026 Lebanon war]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=15 April 2026 |title=Saudi Arabia-hosted Asian Cup draw rescheduled due to US–Israel war on Iran |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/4/15/saudi-arabia-hosted-asian-cup-draw-rescheduled-due-to-us-israel-war-on-iran |access-date=5 June 2026 |publisher=[[Al Jazeera English]]}}</ref> In the decisive fixture against Yemen, Lebanon lost 2–0 and failed to qualify for the Asian Cup.<ref name="gahwashi" />

==Team image==

===Nickname===
Lebanon is known as "the Cedars" ({{langx|ar|رجال الأرز}}) by fans and the media, since the cedar tree is the country's national symbol.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://almustaqbal.com/article/2112057/%D8%B1%D9%8A%D8%A7%D8%B6%D8%A9/%D9%84%D8%A8%D9%86%D8%A7%D9%86/%D8%B1%D8%AC%D8%A7%D9%84-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%A3%D8%B1%D8%B2-%D9%82%D8%A7%D8%AF%D9%85%D9%88%D9%86-%D9%82%D9%84%D9%88%D8%A8%D9%86%D8%A7-%D9%85%D8%B9-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%A3%D8%A8%D8%B7%D8%A7%D9%84!|date=9 January 2019|website=Mustaqbal Web|language=ar|script-title=ar:"رجال الأرز قادمون".. قلوبنا مع "الأبطال"!|trans-title="The Cedars are coming"... our hearts are with "the heroes"!|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190112195307/https://almustaqbal.com/article/2112057/%D8%B1%D9%8A%D8%A7%D8%B6%D8%A9/%D9%84%D8%A8%D9%86%D8%A7%D9%86/%D8%B1%D8%AC%D8%A7%D9%84-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%A3%D8%B1%D8%B2-%D9%82%D8%A7%D8%AF%D9%85%D9%88%D9%86-%D9%82%D9%84%D9%88%D8%A8%D9%86%D8%A7-%D9%85%D8%B9-%D8%A7%D9%84%D8%A3%D8%A8%D8%B7%D8%A7%D9%84!|archive-date=12 January 2019|access-date=12 January 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.pressreader.com/lebanon/the-daily-star-lebanon/20180327/281496456831546|title=Cedars close to qualifying unbeaten for Asian Cup|date=27 March 2018|work=[[The Daily Star (Lebanon)|The Daily Star Lebanon]]|access-date=1 January 2019}}</ref>

===Kits===
{{multiple image
| align     = right
| direction = horizontal
| caption_align = center
| header    = Lebanon's kit over the years
| image1    = Camille Cordahi v Mandatory Palestine, 1940 (crop).jpg
| width1    = 110
| caption1  = 1940
| alt1      = Camille Cordahi wearing a white Lebanese shirt with black shorts
| image2    = Joseph Abou Mrad Lebanon, 1966 (colour).jpg
| width2    = 110
| caption2  = 1966
| alt2      = Joseph Abou Mrad wearing a red Lebanese jersey with a green cedar in the center inside a white horizontal band
| image3    = Felix Michel Melki 20191201.jpg
| width3    = 110
| caption3  = 2019
| alt3      = Felix Michel Melki wearing a red Lebanon shirt with white collar and details
}}
The national team traditionally wear red as their primary colour and white as their secondary colour.{{sfn|Henshaw|1979|p=420}}<ref>{{Cite web|title=Lebanon Team Profile|url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/team/soccer/lebanon/16452/|website=Global Sports Archive|access-date=24 March 2020}}</ref> The choices originate from the [[Flag of Lebanon|national flag of Lebanon]] (red, white, and green); green is sometimes reserved for the goalkeeper.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.elsport.com/news/show/460832/خاص--مهدي-خليل-قدمنا-مباريات-جيدة-واكتسبنا-خبرة-كب|date=23 November 2018|website=Elsport News|language=ar|script-title=ar:خاص- مهدي خليل: قدمنا مباريات جيدة واكتسبنا خبرة كبيرة|trans-title=Special- Mehdi Khalil: We played good games and gained a lot of experience|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref> At home, Lebanon usually wear a red shirt, shorts, and socks, with white details;<ref name="AFC19KSA" /> the away kit is a white outfit with red details.<ref name="AFC19QAT" />

During their first unofficial match in 1935, Lebanon wore white shirts with the Lebanese cedar and the association's name on the chest, black shorts, and white socks; the goalkeeper wore a black shirt and white trousers.{{sfn|Sakr|1992|p=24}} In 1940, on the occasion of their [[1940 Mandatory Palestine v Lebanon football match|first FIFA-sanctioned game]] against [[Mandatory Palestine national football team|Mandatory Palestine]], Lebanon wore a white kit with a black collar, along with black shorts and striped socks.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.football.org.il/magazine/?itemid=%7BC4945714-86D6-4729-8394-0D783EE5BB20%7D|last=Goldberg|first=Asher|date=15 March 2012|publisher=[[Israel Football Association]]|language=he|script-title=he:נבחרת לבנון בתל-אביב|trans-title=Lebanon team in Tel Aviv|access-date=24 March 2020}}</ref> During the 1960s, Lebanon wore a red shirt with a white horizontal band in the center, which included a green [[Cedrus libani|cedar tree]] in the middle; the shorts were white, and the socks were red-and-white-striped.<ref>{{Cite web|title=joseph abou mrad|url=https://www.abdogedeon.com/volleyball/NOUJOUM/joseph%20abou%20mrad.html|website=abdogedeon.com|access-date=24 May 2020|archive-date=5 June 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230605010149/https://www.abdogedeon.com/volleyball/NOUJOUM/joseph%20abou%20mrad.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>

In the [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000 Asian Cup]], Lebanon wore a red [[Adidas]] shirt with white details on the sides and a white collar, white shorts, and red socks.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Lebanon's National Soccer team Asia Cup 2000|url=https://www.upi.com/News_Photos/view/upi/f612d71fece49c261da126c3aac452f2/Asia-Cup/|publisher=[[United Press International]]|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref> In the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019 campaign]], Lebanon wore a red kit with white details and a white collar,<ref name="AFC19KSA" /> manufactured by [[Capelli Sport]], a sports brand founded by Lebanese-born entrepreneur George Altirs.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 February 2019 |title=Capelli Sport – Teams, Kit Designs, Teamwear & History {{!}} Small Brands Case Study |url=https://www.footyheadlines.com/2019/02/capelli-sport-teams-kit-designs-history.html |access-date=11 June 2019 |website=Footy Headlines}}</ref> The [[Cedrus libani|Lebanese cedar]], the country's national symbol, was present under the team logo in a darker shade of red.<ref>{{Cite web|title=LEBANON NATIONAL TEAM|url=https://www.capellisport.com/lebanon-national-team|publisher=[[Capelli Sport]]|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref> Since 2023, the team kit has been manufactured by [[Kelme (company)|Kelme]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Lebanon Kit History |url=https://www.footballkitarchive.com/lebanon-kits/ |access-date=13 June 2024 |website=Football Kit Archive}}</ref> Previous manufacturers include [[Diadora]] and Adidas.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Oumari relishing shot at redemption|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/news/oumari-relishing-shot-at-redemption-2676045|date=7 September 2015|publisher=[[FIFA]]|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Match-fixing scandal shames Lebanese football|url=https://www.dailystar.com.lb/Sports/Football/2013/Feb-27/208084-scandal-shames-lebanese-football.ashx|last=Jabra|first=James|date=27 February 2013|publisher=[[The Daily Star (Lebanon)|The Daily Star]]|access-date=24 May 2020|archive-date=12 November 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201112022438/http://dailystar.com.lb/Sports/Football/2013/Feb-27/208084-scandal-shames-lebanese-football.ashx|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Home stadium===
[[File:Camille Chamoun Sports City Stadium 2018 - Beirut derby (Nejmeh fans).png|thumb|The [[Camille Chamoun Sports City Stadium]] in 2018|alt=Nejmeh fans at the Camille Chamoun Sports City Stadium]]
The Lebanese national team play their home games in various stadiums throughout the country. The team's main venue is the [[Camille Chamoun Sports City Stadium]]. Built in 1957 during the presidency of [[Camille Chamoun]], it is the country's largest stadium with 49,500 seats.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://camillechamounsportscity.com/about/|title=About CCSC|publisher=[[Camille Chamoun Sports City Stadium|Camille Chamoun Sports City]]|access-date=24 March 2020|archive-date=3 January 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190103025007/https://camillechamounsportscity.com/about/|url-status=dead}}</ref> Its inaugural game was in 1957, when the national team played [[FC Petrolul Ploiești|Energia Flacara Ploiesti]] and won 1–0 thanks to a [[Joseph Abou Mrad]] goal.<ref name="CSSC" /> It was the main stadium used to host the [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000 Asian Cup]] held in Lebanon; six matches were played in the stadium including the opening match and the [[2000 AFC Asian Cup Final|final]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.weltfussball.de/spielbericht/asian-cup-2000-im-libanon-gruppe-a-libanon-iran/|title=Libanon – Iran 0:4 (Asian Cup 2000 Libanon, Gruppe A)|website=weltfussball.de|language=de|access-date=1 April 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.weltfussball.de/spielbericht/asian-cup-2000-im-libanon-endspiel-japan-saudi-arabien/|title=Japan – Saudi-Arabien 1:0 (Asian Cup 2000 Libanon, Finale)|website=weltfussball.de|language=de|access-date=1 April 2019}}</ref> In 2011 the stadium hosted the famed 2–1 victory against [[South Korea national football team|South Korea]] in the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC third round|2014 World Cup qualification]], sending Lebanon to the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC fourth round|final round]] of qualification for the first time; over 40,000 spectators were present to watch the match.<ref name="spectators" />

The national team, however, also play in other stadiums such as the [[Saida Municipal Stadium]] located in [[Sidon]]. Built over the sea, the stadium holds 22,600 people,<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://stadiumdb.com/stadiums/lib/saida_international_stadium|title=Saida International Stadium|website=StadiumDB.com|access-date=8 January 2019}}</ref> and was one of the venues to host the [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000 Asian Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.national-football-teams.com/stadium/2358/Saida_International_Stadium.html|title=Saida International Stadium|last=Strack-Zimmermann|first=Benjamin|website=National Football Teams|access-date=23 June 2019}}</ref> Other stadiums in which the national team play include the [[Tripoli Municipal Stadium]] and the [[Beirut Municipal Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tripoli Municipal Stadium|url=http://www.national-football-teams.com/stadium/2606/Tripoli_Municipal_Stadium.html|last=Strack-Zimmermann|first=Benjamin|website=National Football Teams|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Beirut Municipal Stadium|url=http://www.national-football-teams.com/stadium/2998/Beirut_Municipal_Stadium.html|last=Strack-Zimmermann|first=Benjamin|website=National Football Teams|access-date=24 May 2020}}</ref>

===Media===
Produced by [[Fulwell 73]], FIFA released ''Captains'' in 2022, an eight-part sports [[docuseries]] following six national team captains in their respective 2022 World Cup qualification campaigns.<ref name="FIFA+">{{Cite web |date=12 April 2022 |title=FIFA launches FIFA+ to bring free football entertainment to fans everywhere |url=https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/organisation/media-releases/fifa-launches-fifa-to-bring-free-football-entertainment-to-fans-everywhere |access-date=17 October 2022 |publisher=[[FIFA]]}}</ref> Hassan Maatouk, representing Lebanon, starred in the first season alongside [[Thiago Silva]] ([[Brazil national football team|Brazil]]), [[Luka Modrić]] ([[Croatia national football team|Croatia]]), [[Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang]] ([[Gabon national football team|Gabon]]), [[Andre Blake]] ([[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]]) and [[Brian Kaltak]] ([[Vanuatu national football team|Vanuatu]]).<ref name="FIFA+" /> It was released by [[Netflix]], and also shown on FIFA's own streaming platform, [[FIFA+]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Zaazaa |first=Bassam |date=14 October 2022 |title=Lebanon's captain Hassan Maatouk to star in Netflix football documentary |url=https://www.arabnews.com/node/2181136/sport |access-date=17 October 2022 |publisher=[[Arab News]]}}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{see also|Lebanon national football team results|Lebanon national football team records and statistics}}

{{As of|2026|6|4|df=y}}, the Lebanon national football team has played [[Lebanon national football team results|410 official matches]], recording 119 wins, 105 draws, and 186 defeats.<ref name="results" /> The team has scored 492 goals while conceding 610 during this period. Lebanon's largest victory margin remains their 11–1 win against the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] in 1967.<ref name="results" /> The team's longest winning streak stands at eight matches, while their record unbeaten run spans 16 consecutive official games.<ref name="Morrison" />

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

==Coaching staff==
{{Main|List of Lebanon national football team managers}}

[[File:Madjid Bougherra, 2021 FIFA Arab Cup (cropped 2).jpg|thumb|upright|[[Madjid Bougherra]] has been Lebanon's head coach since 2026|alt=Madjid Bougherra wearing a black Adidas sweater during a press conference]]

! Position
! Name

|-

| {{flagicon|LBN}} [[Charbel Krayem]]
|-

| {{flagicon|LBN}} [[Chafik Fares]]
|}

==Players==
{{See also|List of Lebanon international footballers}}

===Current squad===
On 20 May 2026, the following players were named in the squad for the [[2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2027 AFC Asian Cup qualification]] match against [[Yemen national football team|Yemen]] on 4&nbsp;June.<ref>{{Cite web|date=20 May 2026|script-title=ar:بوقرة يسمّي تشكيلته الأولى لمواجهة اليمن الحاسمة|trans-title=Bougherra names his first lineup for the crucial match against Yemen|url=https://the-lfa.com.lb/news/ACQ_m6.php|access-date=20 May 2026|publisher=[[Lebanese Football Association]]|language=ar}}</ref>
{{block indent|left=1.5|1=''Information correct as of 6&nbsp;July 2026.''<ref>{{Cite web |title=Lebanon Current Squad |url=https://www.falebanon.com/list/lebanon-current-squad/ |access-date=2 October 2025 |website=FA Lebanon}}</ref>}}

{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs end}}

The following footballers were part of a national selection in the past 12 months, but are not part of the current squad.

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamad Baker El Housseini]]|sortname=Housseini, Mohamad Baker|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2002|12|18}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Borneo F.C. Samarinda|Borneo Samarinda]]|clubnat=IDN|latest={{sort|2025-09-08|v. {{fb|IDN}}; 8 September 2025}}}}
{{nat fs break}}

{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Malek Fakhro]]|sortname=Fakhro, Malek|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1997|12|14}}|caps=12|goals=5|club=[[1. FC Bocholt]]|clubnat=GER|latest={{sort|2025-11-26|v. {{fb|SUD}}; 26 November 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Samy Merheg]]|sortname=Merheg, Samy|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2006|12|6}}|caps=12|goals=7|club=[[SC Braga|Braga]]|clubnat=POR|latest={{sort|2025-11-26|v. {{fb|SUD}}; 26 November 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ali Kassas]]|sortname=Kassas, Ali|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2003|2|25}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Nejmeh SC|Nejmeh]]|clubnat=LBN|latest={{sort|2025-11-26|v. {{fb|SUD}}; 26 November 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Hussein Ezeddine]]|sortname=Ezeddine, Hussein|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2002|10|17}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Al Ahed FC|Ahed]]|clubnat=LBN|latest={{sort|2025-11-26|v. {{fb|SUD}}; 26 November 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Husseyn Chakroun]]|sortname=Chakroun, Husseyn|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2004|11|10}}|caps=6|goals=2|club=[[Hannover 96]]|clubnat=GER|latest={{sort|2025-10-14|v. {{fb|BHU}}; 14 October 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Daniel Kuri]]|sortname=Kuri, Daniel|age={{birth date and age|df=y|1999|1|22}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[Roda JC Kerkrade|Roda JC]]|clubnat=NED|latest={{sort|2025-09-08|v. {{fb|IDN}}; 8 September 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Karim Mekkaoui]]|sortname=Mekkaoui, Karim|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2001|4|19}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Ethnikos Achna FC|Ethnikos Achna]]|clubnat=CYP|latest={{sort|2025-09-08|v. {{fb|IDN}}; 8 September 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mohammad Al Massri]]|sortname=Massri, Mohammad|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2001|6|22}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Al Ahed FC|Ahed]]|clubnat=LBN|latest={{sort|2025-09-08|v. {{fb|IDN}}; 8 September 2025}}}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ramy Najjarine]]|sortname=Najjarine, Ramy|age={{birth date and age|df=y|2000|4|23}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Wellington Phoenix FC|Wellington Phoenix]]{{efn|name=wel|[[Wellington Phoenix FC|Wellington Phoenix]] is a New Zealand club playing in the [[Australian soccer league system|Australian league system]].}}|clubnat=AUS|latest={{sort|2025-09-08|v. {{fb|IDN}}; 8 September 2025}}}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Individual records==

''FA Lebanon''. Retrieved 11 June 2024.</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last1=Mahfoud |first1=Maroun |last2=Mamrud |first2=Roberto |date=21 March 2024 |title=Lebanon – Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/leb-recintlp.html |access-date=11 June 2024 |website=[[RSSSF]]}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Lebanon.''

==Competitive record==
{{see also|Lebanon national football team results}}

|}

===FIFA World Cup===
[[File:Lebanon v South Korea, 14 November 2019 19.jpg|thumb|Lebanon's match against [[South Korea national football team|South Korea]] at the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification|2022 World Cup qualifiers]]]]
Although the [[Lebanese Football Association]] was formed in 1933,<ref name="akhbar2018" /><ref name="LFAabout" /> Lebanon's first qualification campaign for the [[FIFA World Cup]] took place in the [[1986 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|1986 edition]].<ref name="WC86" /> However, after playing four matches, Lebanon withdrew due to the ongoing [[Lebanese Civil War|civil war]], and their results were subsequently annulled.<ref name="WC86" /> The country's first full qualification campaign came two editions later, in [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|1994]], where they finished third in their group with two wins, four draws, and two losses.<ref name="WC94" /> Ever since, Lebanon have participated in every iteration of the World Cup qualifiers.

Lebanon first reached the final round of World Cup qualification during the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2014 campaign]]. After beating [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]] 4–2 on aggregate in the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC second round|second round]],<ref name="WCqual14BAN" /> Lebanon qualified to the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC third round|third round]], where they were drawn with [[South Korea national football team|South Korea]], [[Kuwait national football team|Kuwait]], and the [[United Arab Emirates national football team|United Arab Emirates]].<ref name="SWWCqual14" /> The team beat South Korea in a historic 2–1 win at home, coming second in their group and qualifying to the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC fourth round|fourth (and final) round]] for the first time.<ref name="BRSK14" /> Grouped with [[Iran national football team|Iran]], South Korea, [[Uzbekistan national football team|Uzbekistan]], and [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]],<ref name="SWAFCqual15" /> Lebanon finished last in Group A and were eliminated with only one win and two draws in eight games.<ref name="SNetSK18" />

{{Lebanon FIFA World Cup record}}

===AFC Asian Cup===
{{main|Lebanon at the AFC Asian Cup}}
[[File:Qatar v Lebanon, 9 January 2019.jpg|thumb|Lebanon's match against [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]] at the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|2019 Asian Cup]]]]
Lebanon's first qualification campaign for the [[AFC Asian Cup|Asian Cup]] came at the [[1972 AFC Asian Cup qualification|1972 edition]]; drawn in Group B of the Western Zone, Lebanon came second thanks to a 3–2 victory over neighbors [[Syria national football team|Syria]] and advanced to the next stage.<ref name="ANC72" /> In the decisive semi-final match against [[Iraq national football team|Iraq]], Lebanon lost 4–1 and were knocked-out.<ref name="ANC72" /> Lebanon won a consolatory third-place match against [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]].<ref name="ANC72" />

The [[2000 AFC Asian Cup|2000 edition]] was Lebanon's first participation in the finals, when the country hosted the event.<ref name="guardian2000AFC" /> Following a 4–0 defeat to [[Iran national football team|Iran]] in the competition's opening match,<ref name="ANC00" /> Lebanon came from behind to draw 2–2 against Iraq;<ref name="ANC00" /> [[Abbas Chahrour]] became Lebanon's first goalscorer in the competition.<ref name="ANC00" /> Lebanon drew once again, 1–1 against [[Thailand national football team|Thailand]], and were eliminated, finishing last in the group.<ref name="ANC00" />

After finishing the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup qualification – third round|2019 third round of qualification]] unbeaten, Lebanon qualified to the Asian Cup for the first time in their history.<ref name="FIFAradu17" /> In the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup|finals]], Lebanon lost the first group stage match 2–0 to eventual champions [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]],<ref name="AFC19QAT" /> before losing once again by the same score to [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]].<ref name="AFC19KSA" /> In the final match of the group, Lebanon needed a win by four goals or more against [[North Korea national football team|North Korea]] to qualify to the knock-out stage.<ref name="AFC19PRK" /> Despite conceding an early [[Free kick (association football)|free-kick]] goal, Lebanon went on to win the match 4–1 thanks to a [[Hat-trick|brace]] by [[Hilal El-Helwe]].<ref name="AFC19PRK" /> However, they lost out to [[Vietnam national football team|Vietnam]] in the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup#Ranking of third-placed teams|third-place ranking]] due to having received more [[Fouls and misconduct (association football)|yellow cards]], and were knocked out of the competition.<ref name="AFC19PRK" />

{{Lebanon AFC Asian Cup record}}

===FIFA Arab Cup===
[[File:Lebanon national football team 1966.jpg|thumb|Lebanon at the [[1966 Arab Cup]]|alt=Eleven Lebanese football players posing for a photo prior to a football match]]
Lebanon have taken part in all iterations of the [[Arab Cup]], except the [[1985 Arab Cup|1985]] and [[1992 Arab Cup|1992]] editions. They hosted the inaugural edition in [[1963 Arab Cup|1963]], in a group containing [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]], [[Syria national football team|Syria]], [[Kuwait national football team|Kuwait]], and [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]].<ref name="AC63" /> After beating Kuwait 6–0 through a [[hat-trick]] by [[Mardik Tchaparian]],<ref name="orient63" /> Lebanon lost 3–2 to Syria, before winning 5–0 against Jordan.<ref name="AC63" /> In a decisive match against Tunisia, [[Muhieddine Itani]] scored an [[own goal]], and Lebanon lost 1–0, finishing third.<ref name="AC63" />

Lebanon finished in fourth place in the subsequent two editions ([[1964 Arab Cup|1964]] and [[1966 Arab Cup|1966]]); ever since, they have failed to pass the group stage.<ref name="AC64" /><ref name="AC66" />

{{Lebanon FIFA Arab Cup record}}

===WAFF Championship===
Bar the [[2008 WAFF Championship|2008]] and [[2010 WAFF Championship|2010]] editions, Lebanon have participated in every [[WAFF Championship]]; they have failed to qualify past the group stage on all occasions. Their first participation in the WAFF Championship was in [[2000 WAFF Championship|2000]], at the inaugural edition.<ref name="WAFF00" /> Drawn with [[Iraq national football team|Iraq]], hosts [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]], and [[Kyrgyzstan national football team|Kyrgyzstan]], Lebanon finished third in their group with one win, one draw, and one loss.<ref name="WAFF00" />

{{Lebanon WAFF Championship record}}

===Olympic Games===
{{see also|Lebanon at the Olympics}}
Lebanon's senior team have never qualified to the [[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic Games]] final tournament; their first qualification campaign was for [[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics – Men's qualification|Rome 1960]].<ref name="OLY60" /> After losing the first two group stage games against [[Iraq national football team|Iraq]], Lebanon withdrew and the two remaining matches were awarded to their opponent [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]].<ref name="OLY60" /> Lebanon participated in two more qualifications, [[Football at the 1968 Summer Olympics – Men's qualification|1968 Mexico City]] and [[Football at the 1972 Summer Olympics – Men's qualification|1972 Munich]], failing to qualify to the final tournament on both occasions.<ref name="OLY68" /><ref name="OLY72" /> Starting from the 1992 edition, the Olympic Football Tournament has been reserved for national under-23 teams.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Aarhus |first=Lars |date=23 September 2021 |title=Football Tournament of the Olympic Games – Overview |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/olympics.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220923151358/https://www.rsssf.org/tableso/olympics.html |archive-date=23 September 2022 |access-date=21 December 2022 |website=[[RSSSF]]}}</ref>

{{Lebanon national football team at the Summer Olympics record}}

===Asian Games===
The Lebanon national senior team only participated once at the [[Football at the Asian Games|Asian Games]], at [[Football at the 1998 Asian Games – Men|Bangkok 1998]]. Thanks to a 5–1 win against [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]], Lebanon qualified past the preliminary round and were drawn with [[Qatar national football team|Qatar]], [[Thailand national football team|Thailand]], and [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] in the second round.<ref name="AG98" /> Following two 1–0 defeats, respectively to Qatar and Thailand, Lebanon won 3–0 against Kazakhstan in their final encounter of the group stage.<ref name="AG98" /> However, the three points were not enough to qualify Lebanon to the knockout round.<ref name="AG98" />

{{Lebanon national football team at the Asian Games record}}

===Arab Games===
After participating in the inaugural edition of the [[Football at the Arab Games|Arab Games]], at [[Football at the 1953 Arab Games|Alexandria 1953]],<ref name="PAG1" /> Lebanon hosted the [[Football at the 1957 Arab Games|1957 edition]].<ref name="PAG2" /> Topping a group containing [[Syria national football team|Syria]], [[Saudi Arabia national football team|Saudi Arabia]], and [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]], Lebanon reached the semi-finals where they lost 4–2 to [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]].<ref name="PAG2" /> Due to [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] withdrawing from the third-place match, Lebanon finished the tournament in third place.<ref name="PAG2" /> Lebanon also came third in [[Football at the 1997 Arab Games|1997]], once again as hosts.<ref name="PAG8" /> With two draws and a win, Lebanon came second in their group and qualified to the semi-finals, which they lost [[Overtime (sports)|after extra time]] to Syria.<ref name="PAG8" /> Lebanon finished in third place after beating [[Kuwait national football team|Kuwait]] 3–1.<ref name="PAG8" />

{{Lebanon national football team at the Arab Games record}}

===Mediterranean Games===
Lebanon's first participation at the [[Football at the Mediterranean Games|Mediterranean Games]] was in [[Football at the 1959 Mediterranean Games|1959]], when they hosted the event.<ref name="MED59" /> They lost both legs against [[Italy national amateur football team|Italy]] and [[Turkey national amateur football team|Turkey]], finishing last with no points.<ref name="MED59" /> Lebanon's senior team participated two more times, in [[Football at the 1963 Mediterranean Games|1963]] and [[Football at the 1987 Mediterranean Games|1987]], failing to qualify past the group stage on both occasions.<ref name="MED63" /><ref name="MED87" />

{{Lebanon national football team at the Mediterranean Games record}}

===Other tournaments===
Lebanon won their first tournament—albeit unofficial—at the [[1964 Tripoli Fair Tournament]]; with three wins and one draw, Lebanon finished first in a group containing Libya, Morocco, Sudan, and Malta.<ref name="FIFAbulletin42" /> In 1998, Lebanon participated at the [[1998 Friendship Tournament|Friendship Tournament]] in the United Arab Emirates where, with two draws and a defeat, they finished in third place out of four.<ref name="friend98" /> Lebanon also finished in third place at the [[2009 King's Cup]] in Thailand; after losing to the hosts in the semi-finals, they won against North Korea in the third-place match.<ref name="kings09" /> Between 2023 and 2024, Lebanon participated in several friendly tournaments, finishing runners-up in the [[2023 Intercontinental Cup (India)|2023 Intercontinental Cup]]<ref name="intercontinental23" /> and the [[2024 Merdeka Tournament]],<ref name="merdeka24">{{Cite web |date=8 September 2024 |title=Malaysia Beat Lebanon 1–0 In Final Of 2024 Merdeka Tournament |url=https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php/news.php?id=2338337 |access-date=13 September 2025 |publisher=[[Bernama]]}}</ref> and in third place in the [[2023 King's Cup]].<ref name="kings23" />

{{Color box|gold|border=darkgray}} Champions&nbsp;&nbsp;{{Color box|silver|border=darkgray}} Runners-up&nbsp;&nbsp;{{Color box|#cc9966|border=darkgray}} Third place&nbsp;&nbsp;

;Bibliography
